text:
Admission Date:  2016-04-01              Discharge Date:   2008-06-17

Date of Birth:  1978-05-15             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Morphine Sulfate

Attending:Keith
Chief Complaint:
fall, leg fracture

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
ORIF


History of Present Illness:
Surgery Admission:  41-year-old man with metastatic renal cell
carcinoma, mechanical aortic valve on warfarin, who was
transferred from OSH after an unwitnessed fall at rehab,
fracturing his left femur. Patient fell out of bed this morning
while trying to get to the bathroom. He stated that he tripped
over something. Denied hitting his head. Denied palpitations,
chest pain, dizziness, or any other symptom before the fall. He
was brought to Sjrh - Park Care Pavilion where he was diagnosed with
a midshaft left femur fracture. Got vitamin K 10 mg PO x 1 for
?INR > 5 (no record available). Reported received his outpatient
Keppra dose. Patient was put in hard traction and transferred to
Menlo Park Surgical Hospital for further management.
.
On arrival here, T 98.2, HR 73, BP 120/80, RR 16, 95%RA. INR was
3.5. His hip, femur, and knee films confirmed the left midshaft
femur fracture. Head and C-spine CT were unremarkable.
Orthopedics placed a long leg splint, planned to take him to the
OR tomorrow for ORIF, recommended anticoagulation reversal for
procedure with FFP and vitamin K. In the ED, he received his
scheduled lamotrogine 125 mg, gabapentin 1200 mg, zonisamide 200
mg, as well as hydromorphone 1 mg IV for pain control. Admitted
to OMED for further management.
.
Review of Systems:
(+) Per HPI
(-) Denies fever, chills, night sweats, recent weight loss or
gain. Denies headache, sinus tenderness, rhinorrhea or
congestion. Denies chest pain or tightness, palpitations. Denies
cough, shortness of breath, or wheezes. Denies nausea, vomiting,
diarrhea, constipation or abdominal pain. No recent change in
bowel or bladder habits. No dysuria. Denies arthralgias or
myalgias. Denies rashes or skin breakdown. No numbness/tingling
in extremities. No feelings of depression or anxiety. All other
review of systems negative.

ICU Admission:
Mr. Peterson is a 41yo M with history of renal cell carcinoma
metastatic to brain and lung, and AVR who presented to Menlo Park Surgical Hospital on
06-19 after femur fracture.  He sustained an unwitnessed fall at
rehab and fractured his femur.  He underwent surgical
intramedullary fixation on 11-21, and his course has been
complicated by difficult to control seizure activity and
suspected aspiration pneumonia.
.
After surgery, he had hypoxia requiring NRB which was eventually
weaned to nasal cannula.  Patient had a CTA of his chest on 01-17
which showed ground glass opacity in his right upper and middle
lobes with consolidation of the left lower lobe and possible
right upper lobe segmental artery embolus.  Patient was started
on HCAP coverage with vanc/cefepime/flagyl and continued on
anticoagulation for his AVR and questionable PE.  His
respiratory status improved over the next few days to lower
nasal cannula oxygen requirements and was briefly stable on room
air earlier in the day prior to Genesis Hospital admission.
.
He has had difficult to control seizure activity/periodic
lateralized epileptiform discharges (reportedly seizures start
with rhythmic movements of right hand and progress to aphasia)
from his known brain mets.  Patient has been followed by neuro
oncology and is currently on levetiracetam, zonisamide,
lorazepam, lacosamide, gabapentin, lamictal and PRN ativan for
breakthrough.  He was in the middle of 24 hour continuous EEG
but had some difficult to control seizure activity with
increased hypoxia requiring 10L via facemask and sinus
tachycardia to the 140s.  He was transferred to the Genesis Hospital for
closer monitoring and further management.
.
In the Genesis Hospital, patient is able to answer some questions but has
baseline expressive aphasia and periods of somnolence which
limit his ability to respond.  He denies being in any pain and
reports that his breathing feels ok.
.


Past Medical History:
PAST MEDICAL AND SURGICAL HISTORY:
Oncologic History from Dr.Dustin Hall notes:
(1) Radical left nephrecotmy by Teresa Williamson, M.D. at the Kahi Mohala
and Woman's Hospital showing renal cell carcinoma, 1984.
(2) Resection of a dura-based mass on the right side of his head
in 1990-08-14 by Brian Beck, M.D. at the Kahi Mohala and Woman's
Hospital.
(3) Second craniotomy surgery to evacuate a subdural hematoma
(4) Third craniectomy for Staphylococcus aureus infection.
(5) Cyberknife radiosurgery on 1998-06-11 to 1-right parietal,
2-left medial parietal, and 3-left lateral parietal metastases,
all to 2200 cGy at 76% isodose line.
(6) Cyberknife radiosurgery on 1983-07-14 to 4-left frontal,
5-right occipital, and 6-left posterior parietal metastases to
2,200 cGy at 76% isodose line.
(7) Also underwent high-dose interleukin 2 therapy in 1989.
(8) Started [**Year (4 digits) 81647**] on 1984-10-08.

Other Significant History:
(1) Ascending aortic aneurysm repair in 1988-06-09
(2) Mechanical aortic valve replacement in 1979-10-03 on
chronic anticoagulation with Coumadin (Summa Health System Barberton Hospital, goal INR 2 -
2.5
at present)
(3) Status post ischemic bowel resection in 1989 secondary to a
clot after his aortic valve replacement while not adequately
anticoagulated
(4) Appendectomy at age 8.

Social History:
He taught at the 84086 Kristina Mountain
South Sherryhaven, WV 94493 of 177 Angela Trafficway Apt. 229
North Caitlin, MD 12822, formerly a history
professor, but now not working, given metastatic renal cell.  He
is married and has three children.  He smoked 1 pack of
cigarettes per day for 15 years but stopped in 1973.  He does
not drink alcohol or use illicit drugs. Formerly drank socially.


Family History:
There was questionable lung cancer in his maternal
great-grandmother. His mother is 69 and she is healthy.  His
father is 79 and he is healthy.  He has a sister who is healthy.
 His three children are healthy.


Physical Exam:
General: alert and oriented, conversational

HEENT: atraumatic, eyes not deviated, oropharynx clear

Neck: supple

Lungs: poor inspiratory effort, clear to auscultation anteriorly
and in axillae

CV: regular rate and rhythm, normal S1, mechanical S2, no
murmurs, rubs, gallops

Abdomen: soft, obese, NT, non-distended, bowel sounds present,
no rebound tenderness or guarding, no organomegaly

Ext: warm, well perfused, 1+ pedal edema

Neuro: alert and oriented, able to move extremities on command


Pertinent Results:
2016-04-01 06:40PM   WBC-6.9 RBC-3.77* HGB-9.9* HCT-31.3* MCV-83
MCH-26.3* MCHC-31.7 RDW-21.0*
2016-04-01 06:40PM   NEUTS-96* BANDS-2 LYMPHS-1* MONOS-0 EOS-0
BASOS-0 ATYPS-0 METAS-1* MYELOS-0
2016-04-01 06:40PM   PLT COUNT-345
2016-04-01 06:40PM   PT-34.3* PTT-26.7 INR(PT)-3.5*
2016-04-01 06:40PM   GLUCOSE-138* UREA N-19 CREAT-0.7 SODIUM-139
POTASSIUM-4.5 CHLORIDE-106 TOTAL CO2-26
2016-04-01 06:40PM   CALCIUM-8.6 MAGNESIUM-2.1
2016-04-01 06:40PM   OSMOLAL-294
.
IMAGING:
# Head CT w/o contrast 06-19: (prelim) No acute hemorrhage. Areas
of hypodensity, encephalomalacia in bilateral parietal lobes,
chronic in appearance. Ex vacuo dilation left lateral ventricle.

# C-spine CT w/o contrast 06-19: (prelim) No acute fracture or
malalignment.
# L hip, femur, knee Xray 06-19: (prelim) acute fx involving
mid-femur, displaced

Brief Hospital Course:
41-year-old man with metastatic renal cell carcinoma, mechanical
aortic valve on warfarin, presented with left femur fracture
after a fall.
.
# Left femur fracture: s/p ORIF. No neurological or vascular
compromise on exam. Pt was taken to the OR by ortho on 11-21 with
ORIF performed and INR reveresed prior to proceedure. Pt in
splint after proceedure and recovered appropriately. Pain
control with dilaudid and tylenol. Staples removed prior to
discharge.
.
# Metastatic renal cell carcinoma: with brain mets, complicated
by seizures and aphasia. Pt on complicated seizure regimine
which was continued as below. Dexamethasone was continued and
neuro/oncology was contactVirginia Blair regarding initiation of further
chem/thoughts on prognosis. Neuro-onc met with family and
recommended palliative care consult in context of poor prognosis
and family variance in opinion about how situation should be
managed.  Patient was made DNR/DNI and will be discharged to
hospice.  Decision was made not to initiate tube feeds or TPN.
.
# H/o seizures: had multiple seizures during last admission.
Complex antiepileptic regimen with levetiracetam, zonisamide,
lorazepam prn, lacosamide, lamotrigine, and gabapentin.
Initially given these meds with ativan for seizure control. Pt
received continuous EEG on day of ICU transfer. Negative for
seizure activity. Ativan d/ced after EEG data back.  Since
patient was not taking POs due to aspiration risk and lethargy,
his antiepileptic regimen was readjusted to entail IV
medications.  He will be discharged on IV keppra, vimpat, and
lorazepam as well as IV dexamethasone.  He was not observed to
have any seizures on this regimen.
.
# Mechanical aortic valve/supratherapeutic INR: for aortic
stenosis. INR 3.5 after receiving 10 mg of oral vitamin K at
OSH. These processes reversed in order to allow ortho surgery.
Put on lovenox after surgery and continued on this during ICU
course.
.
# Sinus tachycardia: He has had intermittent sinus tachycardia
since admission, sustained in the 120s at time of ICU transfer.
Some concern for PE. LLE U/S negative for PE. Pt continued on
metoprolol and treated for possible infection.
.
# Hypoxia: Increased oxygen requirement on day ICU transfer.
This is concerning for possible repeat aspiration pneumonia or
may be related to questionable PE seen on CTA.  However, the new
acuity of his desaturation would argue for possible new
worsening PE versus the segmental area seen on 01-17.  Pulmonary
edema is also a possibility but his lungs are currently clear
and CXR is not consistent with this. Pt continued on treatment
for HAP and anticoagulation (lovenox) at therapeutic levels.
.
#. Pneumonia, s/p possible aspiration event: CTA 01-17 showed
RUL/RML ground-glass opacities and LLL consolidation. Completed
course of broad coverage with Vanc/Cefepime/Flagyl to treat
aspiration/HAP. Thought that PNA contributing to acute hypoxia.
.

#C diff: positive for C. Diff.  Started on IV Flagyl.  Will
continue for 14 day course.


Medications on Admission:
Amlodipine 5 mg PO DAILY
Acetaminophen 325 mg PO Q6H as needed for pain.
Levetiracetam 1500 mg PO Q 8H
Warfarin 7.5 mg PO Once Daily
Zonisamide 200 mg PO BID
Pantoprazole 40 mg PO Q12H
Lorazepam 0.5 mg Q 8H
Metoprolol Succinate 100 mg PO DAILY
Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID
Senna 8.6 mg PO BID as needed for constipation.
Insulin Regular sliding scale
Quetiapine 37.5 mg PO BID as needed for agitation.
Lacosamide 300 mg PO bid
Dexamethasone 8 mg PO BID
Gabapentin 1200 mg PO Q8H
Lorazepam 1 mg IV as needed for seizure.
Lamictal 150 mg PO twice a day

Discharge Medications:
1. Amlodipine 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily):
hold if patient is not taking POs.
2. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO Q8H (every
8 hours): please give PR if patient unable to take PO.
3. Lacosamide 200 mg/20 mL Solution Sig: Four Hundred (400) mg
Intravenous Dekalb Medical At Decatur (2 times a day).
4. Metronidazole in NaCl (Iso-os) 500 mg/100 mL Piggyback Sig:
Five Hundred (500) mg Intravenous Q8H (every 8 hours) for 12
days.
5. Lorazepam 2 mg/mL Syringe Sig: 1-2 mg Injection PRN (as
needed) as needed for seizure activity.
6. Dexamethasone Sodium Phosphate 4 mg/mL Solution Sig: Eight
(8) mg Injection Q12H (every 12 hours).
7. Pantoprazole 40 mg Recon Soln Sig: One (1) Recon Soln
Intravenous Q12H (every 12 hours).
8. Lorazepam 2 mg/mL Syringe Sig: 0.5 mg Injection Q6H (every 6
hours).
9. Levetiracetam 500 mg/5 mL Solution Sig: 1500 (1500) mg
Intravenous Q 8H (Every 8 Hours).
10. Metoprolol Tartrate 5 mg/5 mL Solution Sig: Five (5) mg
Intravenous Q6H (every 6 hours).
11. Lovenox 120 mg/0.8 mL Syringe Sig: One [**Age over 90 **]y (120)
mg Subcutaneous twice a day.


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Summa Health System Barberton Hospitalhospice

Discharge Diagnosis:
Femur fracture s/p ORIF 2006-02-07
aspiration pneumonia
C. diff infection
Renal cell carcinoma metastatic to brain and lung
seizures


Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Confused - sometimes.
Level of Consciousness: Lethargic but arousable.
Activity Status: Bedbound.


Discharge Instructions:
Dear Mr. Peterson,
You were admitted to the hospital for a femur fracture.  After
surgery, you had diffult to control seizure activity and
suspected aspiration pneumonia.  You were admitted to the
intensive care unit where you were not given anything to eat by
mouth due to concern for aspiration.  Your oral anti-seizure
medications were changed to intravenous medications.  You were
treated with antibiotics for pneumonia.  Your code status was
changed to Do not resuscitate and do not intubate after a family
meeting with the ICU team as well as oncology.  You are being
discharged to hospice level care.  Of note, you also were found
to have an infection called C. Difficile and are being treated
with antibiotics.

Followup Instructions:
Please f/u with your PCP at your discretion.
